def: s.f. A wife.
s.m. (Dakkhanī) = kanauṅṛā, q.v.
s.f. The black aromatic seed of Nigella indica (it is used medicinally; — syn. magrelā).

s. f. The name of a seed used medicinally. (Nigella indica. Roxb. ).

def: s.f. (rustic), Blackness, &c. ( = kālak, q.v.). s.m. Fretfulness, restlessness, disquietude, discomfort, perturbation, agitation, commotion, anxiety, keen regret
subs: (-meṅ), The disquietude, or regret, &c. (of a thing) to remain, or to rankle (in the mind)
or qalaq honā (with gen. of thing, and dat. of person), To feel anxiety, or pain, or sorrow; to regret; to pine (for, or after).

ety: prob. S. कल्मष; or fr. काल
def: s.f. Blackness; stain, spot, impurity, &c. (see kalmash, and kalaṅk, and dāg̠).
more: n. f. 1. Blackness. 2. See dāg , 3. kalauṅs kā ṭīkā . 1. A stigma. 2. A stain. kalauṅs lagnā , v. n. 1. To be blackened. 2. To be stained ( dhabbā lagnā ).

def: s.m. The band or rope round an elephant's neck in which the driver fixes his feet (cf. kalāba, and kalāp).
more: s. (dakh.) A giving to eat, a feeding (?).
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀓𑀮𑀸𑀯 (kalāva), from Sanskrit कलाप (kalāpa). First attested in c. 1603 as Middle Hindi کلاوا (klava). Cognate with Marathi कळवा (kaḷvā), Punjabi کلاوا (kalāvā).

def: s.m. = kalāba, and kalāwā, qq.v. s.m. A raw thread as it is wound from the spindle; raw silk wound on a reel; yarn; a skein or ball of thread, a clew, hank; a reel; a wheel (for winding thread on); — (for kalāwā, q.v.) the band round an elephant's neck in which the driver fixes his feet (syn. kalāwa).
more: n. m. 1. A red skein or hank. 2. A skein of thread. 3. The rope round an elephant's neck.
s. m. A skein or ball of thread; the band round the elephantʼs neck in which the driver fixes his feet. The elephantʼs neck: Gilch.

more: n. m. S. कलावत A professional musician and singer. Gāte gāte kalāvaṅt ho jātā hai . Prov. kalāvaṅt-bachchā , n. m. A minstrel-born.
s. m. A kind of singer or musician.
subs: To make a fortification, or an entrenchment
To build a fort; to fortify (a place)
adj. Shut up in a fort
To take refuge in a fort; to keep in a fortified place (an army)
vulg. qilʻe-dār, s.m. Governor of a fort, commandant of a garrison
s.f. The command of a garrison
s.f. A siege-gun; siege-battery; a very large cannon
s.f. The garrison of a fort.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit कलावत् (kalāvat).

def: s.m. A sparrow.
s.m. A long, thin, and small black bean (very glutinous and nutritious: it is used as food for horses).
more: n. m. A long, thin and small black bean; very glutinous, used as food for horses. (?).

ety: inf. n. of قلب 'to invert, turn over or upside down,' &c.
def: s.m. Turning, changing; turning over or upside down, inverting; inversion; — the heart; the mind; the secret thoughts; the soul; the intellect, intelligence, understanding; — the main body (of an army); — the pure, or choice, or best part (of anything), pith, marrow, kernel; — counterfeit coin; — adj. Inverted; adulterated, counterfeit, bad; — significant of operations of the mind (a verb, e.g. fěʻluʼl-qalb, or fěʻl-ě-qalb); — strongly fortified, impregnable
more: n. m. 1. The heart; mind. 2. The centre or main body (of an army). qalb-sāz , n. m. A counterfeit coiner. qalb-sāzī , n. f. Making counterfeit coin.

1. s. m. The heart. 2. Mind, soul, understanding. 3. Kernel, marrow. 4. The centre or main body (of an army). 5. adj. Inverted. 6. Adulterated, bad. 7. Strongly fortified, impregnable. قلب کرنا ḳalb karnā, To turn over, to invert, to turn. قلب گاه ḳalb-gāh, Place of the heart, middle, the middle or centre (of an army).
subs: s.m. A maker of counterfeit coin, a counterfeit-coiner
v.t. To turn over, to invert, to turn
s.f. Place of the heart; the middle; the middle or centre (of an army).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian قَلْب (qalb), from Arabic قَلْب (qalb).

def: s.m. A dog (syn. sag; kuttā).
wety: Borrowed from English club.

def: s.f. = kalwal, q.v. s.f. Affliction, misfortune, calamity.
s.m. A kind of song or music.
s.m. A variety of the Aloe perfoliata (the Aloe plant from which the aloes of the market are obtained).
more: n. A variety of the Aloe perfoliata , the Aloe plant from which the aloes of the shops are obtained. (?).

def: adj. Of or relating to the heart; hearty, cordial (syn. dilī); — adulterated; false; counterfeit, base (as coin).
more: adj. 1. Hearty; cordial. 2. ( qalb ) Counterfeit; false.

def: s.m. The arabicized form of kālbud, q.v.

ety: for orig. kalbūt, fr. kalb or kalp = Pehl. karp; Zend kehrp (fr. karep = S. kalp); S. कल्प;+but (?), q.v.
def: s.m. The body (of a man or animal); the frame; the heart; — figure, form, mould, model; a last (for shoes).
more: n. m. 1. The human body. 2. (Pop. kālbūt ) A shoemaker's last. kālbūt denā , v. a. To make a last.
s. m. 1. The human body. 2. The heart. 3. A model, figure, form.

def: adj. = kal-jahwāṅ, q.v.

s.m. The liver (commonly applied to the liver of a human being; the fem. kalejī being used for the liver of an animal); the liver, lungs, and heart collectively (syn. jigar-band), the vitals; the heart; stomach, heart, mind, spirit, courage, magnanimity
subs: The heart to bound or leap (with joy, &c.); to be overjoyed; the heart to palpitate
To lose heart, to be anxious or distracted
The heart to come to the mouth; to be exhausted with excessive vomiting
(-ke), To be wounded in the heart; to suffer a heavy shock or blow
To suffer jealousy or envy; to repine
(-kā, or merā, &c.), To be deeply wounded, to be cut to the quick
(kā), The heart to burst; to feel pity or compassion; to be disturbed with grief; to burst with envy or jealousy
(-kā), 'The heart to be fresh'; to be at ease, to be well off
'To press the heart and weep,' to weep bitterly
To suppress (one's) feelings; to repress grief
To satisfy the heart's longing, to obtain (one's) wish; to get ease
(-kā), 'The heart to burn,' to suffer sorrow, to be heart-sore, to mourn
(-ko), To keep next to the heart, to hold very dear, to love exceedingly, to cherish; to caress, embrace
The heart to tremble or palpitate; to feel afraid; to suffer cold
Liver-complaint; heart-disease: kalejā muṅh-ko ānā = kalejā ulaṭnā; — (also) to feel sick (at the sight of), to loathe, abhor
(-ko), To cherish in the heart, to love or esteem exceedingly
or kaleje-khāʼī, s.f. A witch who devours the livers of human beings.
ex: اس کے پاس پتھر کا کلیجا ہے۔
uske pās patthar kā kalejā hai
He has a heart of stone.
تم میرے کلیجے کا ٹکڑا ہو۔
tum mere kaleje kā ṭukṛā ho.
You are very dear to my heart.
wety: From Sanskrit कालेय (kāleya). Compare Bhojpuri 𑂍𑂩𑂵𑂔𑂰 (karejā), Magahi 𑂍𑂩𑂵𑂔 (karej), Assamese কলিজা (koliza), Marathi काळीज (kāḷīj), Bengali কলিজা (kolija), Punjabi کالجا (kāljā).

def: s.m. = kalejā, q.v. s.m. The liver (commonly applied to the liver of a human being; the fem. kalejī being used for the liver of an animal); the liver, lungs, and heart collectively (syn. jigar-band), the vitals; the heart; stomach, heart, mind, spirit, courage, magnanimity
more: n. m. S. कालेय 1. The human liver or heart. 1. Tū to merā kalejā hai . 2. Ghī kahāṅ gayā? Khichṛī meṅ. Khīchṛī kahāṅ gai? Pyāre ke kaleje meṅ . Prov. Where has the butter gone? All in the khichṛī . Where has the mess all gone? Into my loved one's stomach. 2. Courage; spirit; magnanimity. kalejā uchhalnā , v. n. 1. To palpitate ( dhaṛaknā ). 2. To bound or leap (with joy); to be overjoyed. kalejā uṛā jānā , v. n. To be anxious or distracted. kalejā ulaṭnā , v. n. 1. To be exhausted with excessive vomiting. 2. To come to one's mouth (the heart). kalejā baiṭhā jānā , v. n. To sink (one's heart). kalejā bīṅdhnā, chhednā, yā sālnā , v. a. To pierce one's heart. Ek or jomadarvā, ek or sipahiyā, bīch meṅ pīavā mor, Jab piavā ke musak ckaṛhāve, sāle kalejvā mor . E. Wom song. A Sergeant here, a soldier there, My love placed 'twixt the twain, His arms they twist and bind so sair It wrings my hear…

s. m. 1. The liver. 2. ( met. ) Courage, spirit, magnanimity, heart. کليجه الٿنا kaleja ulaṭnā, To be fatigued with excessive vomiting. کليجه پهٿنا kaleja phaṭnā, To be disturbed with grief or jealousy. کليجه ٿهنڐها کرنا kaleja ṭhanḍhā karnā, To obtain oneʼs wish, to get ease. کليجه جلنا کسي کا kaleja jalnā kisī-kā, To suffer sorrow, to mourn. کليجه کانپنا kaleja kāmpnā, To be afraid, to suffer cold. کليجي پرسانپ پهرنا kaleje par sāmp phirnā, To suffer jealousy or envy. کليجي سي لگا رکهنا يا لينا kaleje se lagā-raḵẖnā or -lenā, To caress, embrace, love exceedingly. کليجي مين ڐال رکهنا kaleje meṅ ḍāl raḵẖnā, To love or esteem exceedingly.

def: s.f. The liver (of an animal); the liver, lungs, and heart together (syn. jigar-band).
more: n. f. The liver of animals, used as food.

def: s.m. = kalakṭar, q.v. s.m. Collector (of revenue), the chief revenue officer of a district.
s.m. (rustic), 1˚ = karelā; — 2˚ = kalewā, qq.v. s.m. A kind of bitter vegetable, Momordica charantia; — a sort of fire-works (with or without stars).
s.m. See kleś.
more: n. m. S. केश Pālī. kalaho, kileso , distress: Pr. kileso . 1. Distress ( ranj , 1.) 1. Bam Mahesh! kāṭo kalesh! Religious cry. 2. Hā kā, khāūṅ! koī mere bān se kah de! Meṛe jī kā kales miṭāë de, ri ! Koī maike kī rāh batāë de rī! Wom. song. I pray you, Sir! would some one but my father tell! My anguish wipe, direct me where my parents dwell! 2. Contention; ford ( jhagṛā ). kales k. , v. a. 1. To affect; pain. 2. To strive with; struggle against; to fight. Merā kahnā mān pyāre! kyon jā hai pardes? Sū/?/ sej nīṅd nahiṅ āre joban kare kates Play. List to my words, beloved! from home why wilt thou flee, Leaving an empty couch and languished breasts to me? 3. To suffer grief or pain; to mourn. kales machānā , v. n. To create dissensions, quarrels, etc. kales-hāyā , n. m. kales-hāī , n. f. A. quarrelsome person.
s. m. 1. Sickness, pain, trouble, distress, vexation, affliction, torment. 2. (Hind.) Quarrel, contention.

def: s.m. (poet.) = kaleś or kleś, q.v. s.m. See kleś.
s.m. Name of a drug.
more: s. m. Name of a drug.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -hāʼī), Troublesome, giving annoyance; quarrelsome; — a troublesome person, or a quarrelsome person.
s.m. Name of a drug.
s.m. = kalewā, q.v. s.m. A light morning meal, breakfast; luncheon, stale food
more: s. m. Cold meat, stale victuals, a luncheon, a breakfast.
subs: To make a morning meal, to breakfast, &c.

def: s.m. A light morning meal, breakfast; luncheon, stale food
more: n. m. S. कल्याहार A light breakfast or luncheon. 1. Badrī ke bahuriyā kalerā na kareṅ E. Prov. On a cloudy day the daughter-in-law for breakfast wont get up. 2. Nān bahū, nūn kalevā . Prov. For a tiny daughter-in-law a pinch of salt is a breakfast. (Said sarcastically of her eating so much). kalevā k. , v. a. To breakfast or lunch.
s. m. Cold meat, stale victuals, a luncheon, a breakfast.
subs: To make a morning meal, to breakfast, &c.

def: s.m. The body; a carcass.
more: n. m. S. कलेवर Pālī, kalebanam , A body; carcass.

def: s.m. = کلغا kalg̠ā, q.v.
more: s. m. The name of a flower, cockscomb (Amaranthus), princeʼs feather.

s. (dakh.) An axe, pickaxe ( Harris ).
s.f. = kalgī, q.v.
s.f. = کلغي kalg̠ī, q.v.

ety: for kalgā, kalgī; prob. = S. कलश, कलशी, by change of ś to k, and then to g̠; — P. kalakī
def: s.m. A composer or singer of the kalg̠ī or lāʼonī songs.
more: n. f. 1. ( kalgā ) A crest; the comb (of a cock) 2. The spire on the top of a dome. 3. An ornament on a turban; an aigrette; a plume; a humā , or phœnix feather with a pearl fixed in the turban. 4. A class of songs, as a Lāonī. kal jī-bāz , Composers of Lāonī (songs). There are two classes of Lāonī singers, Turra-bāz and Kalgi-bāz .
s. f. An ornament on the turband, an aigrette, a plume.
s. A humā or phenix feather fixed into the turband, having generally a pearl fastened to the end of it ( Qanoon-e-islam ). The comb (of a cock), crest ( Harris ).

def: adv. Yesterday; to-morrow.
adv. (rustic) = kalh, or kal, q.v.
s.f. A woollen carpet; tapestry ( = qālīn).
more: s. f. A carpet, tapestry. See غالي.

def: s.m. A die; the game of dice personified; name of the last and worst of the four yugas or ages, the present or iron age (according to the Hindūs), the age of vice (the Kali or fourth age contains 432,000 years of men, and is supposed to begin on the 18th February 3102 B.C.; at the end of this yuga the world is to be destroyed); — dissension, strife, contention, quarrel
more: n. f. S. कलिका. 1. A bud; blossom. Tāgā ṭūṭe phir juṛe, kalī ṭūṭ kumlāë, Man kā ṭūṭā ādmī dhore dhore jāë . The broken thread will join, slow fades nipped flowers unblown, The heart of man may break yet brokenly live on. 2. A feather. Kabūtar kī kalī ṭūṭī . 3. Quick lime; wet-lime. 4. A piece in a native coat. 5. A kind of pipe for smoking. kalī barābar . ( Bot .) Regular. kalī be-barābar . ( Bot .) Irregular. kalī chaṛhī-huī . ( Bot .) Imbricated. kalī kā chūnā . Limestone. kaliyāṅ ānā, kaliyānā , v. n. 1. To bud; blossom. 2. To get new feathers. Paṭṭhā kalyā rahā hai. ek kaliyā, milā huā . ( Boṭ .) Mono-sepalous. kachchī yā band kalī , n. f. 1. A. pomegranate bud. 2. A virgin.
s. f. (dakh. s. m.) 1. A bud, an unblown flower, blossom. 2. Quicklime. 3. कलि The kali-yug, or fourth age of the world, according to the Hindūs.
subs: vulg. kal-kāl, s.m. The time or age of Kali, the fourth age (in the Hindū Mythology)
s.m. An epithet of any poet of the fourth or Kali-yug
vulg. kal-mal, s.m. Impurity, guilt, sin
s.f. An epithet of the river Karam-nāsā (q.v.)
vulg. kali-jug, or kal-jug, s.m. The fourth or iron age
vulg. kal-jugī, adj. Of or relating to the fourth or iron age; extending over the period of the fourth age.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀓𑀮𑀺𑀕 (kaliga), from Sanskrit कलिका (kalikā). Cognate with Gujarati કળી (kaḷī), Marathi कळी (kaḷī).

ety: fem. of काल; and of H. kālā
def: s.f. Black colour; blacking; ink; a black spot, a mole, a freckle, &c. ( = kālak); — an epithet of the goddess Durgā, wife of Śiva (the Hecate of the Hindūs, to whom human sacrifices are offered); — the river Nile.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit काली (kālī). Equivalent to کال (kāl) + ـی (-ī).

def: adv. (rustic) = kalh, or kal, q.v.
more: n. f. The goddess Kālī. Kālī Kalkatte-vālī terī sadā jaë! Religious cry. kālī-balā . 1. A plague; a greatevil. 2. An old hag. kālī pīlī āṅkheṅ k. , v. a. To turn red with rage. kālī pīlī chīl chilo , n. f. A game among children. kālī-dah , n. f. A whirlpool in the Jamnā , where the snake Kālī lived. kālī debī , n. f. (Opium eaters) Opium. kālī-zīrī , n. f. Vernonia anthelmintica , Wat. kālī sītlā, yā mātā , n. f. Malignant or confluent small-pox. kālī sem , n. m. A bean; Dolichos villosus . Wat. kālī kuṭkī . Black hellebore; Helleborus niger . kālī kachkūrī , n. Dolichos (Carpopogon) pruriens purpurascens (?), a variety of Cowitch with a purplish stem. kālī kalūṭī, kālā kalūṭā , adj. Black as a lamp-black box; black (person). kāle kos , n. A great distance. Dekhiye, kab Khudā milāëgā! Ab-ke Rangīṅ gaë haiṅ kāle kos! Wom. Rangīn. kālī miṭṭī , n. f. Black soil. kālī mirch , n. f. 1. Black …

1. adj. f. Black. کالي مرچ kālī mirch, s. f. Black pepper. 2. s. f. The Hecate of the Hindūs, and wife of Siva, to whom human sacrifices are offered. 3. The Nile. 4. Ink. 5. s. m. (for कालिय ) Name of a snake with a hundred and ten hoods, vanquished by Kr̤ish̤ṇa. کالي بون kālī-būn, s. m. Name of a plant. کالي تلسي kālī-tulsī, s. f. Basil. (Ocimum sanctum). کالي ده kālī-dah, Name of a whirlpool in the river Jamunā, where the snake کالي lived. کالي زيري kālī-zīrī, s. f. See سوم راج somrāj. کالي مٿي kālī-miṭṭī, s. f. Black lead.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit काली (kālī). Equivalent to کال (kāl) + ـی (-ī).

def: s.f. An unblown flower, a bud, blossom, button (of a flower); — new feather (of a bird); a gusset, a piece put in a garment (to enlarge it); — quick-lime, wet-lime; — a kind of pipe or ḥuqqa (cf. kaliyān)
subs: (-par), To whitewash; to coat with lime
(-meṅ), To have buds; to bud, blossom; — to get new feathers, to begin to be fledged.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀓𑀮𑀺𑀕 (kaliga), from Sanskrit कलिका (kalikā). Cognate with Gujarati કળી (kaḷī), Marathi कळी (kaḷī).

def: s.m. Name of a Nāga or serpent that inhabited the Jamnā (it is said to have had a hundred and ten heads, and to have been slain by Krishṇ)
subs: or kālī-kunḍ, s.m. The pit or whirlpool in the river Jamnā in which the serpent Kālī is said to have lived
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit काली (kālī). Equivalent to کال (kāl) + ـی (-ī).

def: n. m. قلب reversed. 1. Material; frame; body; lifeless form. 2. The heart-shaped block on which tailors cut out caps. 3. ( Arch .) The centering. 4. A last (Cobblers). qālib badalnā ; H. cholā badalnā , v. n. To undergo transmigration; to be transformed.
adj. Cast in a mould, moulded.
more: adj. Cast in a mould, made on a mould, moulded.

def: s.m. A small carpet; a rug.
s. m. A small carpet.

ety: kālī+dāsa, 'the slave of Kālī or Durgā'
def: s.m. Name of a celebrated Hindū poet (the Shakespeare of India; author of the Śakuntalā, Vikramorvaśī, Megha-dūta, Raghu-vaṅśa, Nalodaya, and other poems: he was one of the nine poets or gems of Vikramāditya's court, and is supposed to have flourished in the century preceding the Christian era); a proper name.
n. f. A worm disease in cattle.
(perhaps from काली Black, and हरिणी A doe) s. f. A buck, deer.

Tir. n. f. S. कलह A quarrelsome woman. 1. Tujh kalihārī nār se maiṅ dukhiyā hūṅ āp, Jo tū ghar se nikal jāë miṭeṅ janam ke pāp . Play. My life's a burden made by such a wrangling jade, Would you the house but leave't would be a life's reprieve. 2. Jis ghar ho nārī kalihārī us purkhā kī saë at khuārī . Rus. song. A wrangling woman for a spouse brings ruin on her husband's house. 3. Kad yoh sāsū, rāṅḍ maregī? pothī khol, are pāṅḍe! Jib lag jīve yoh kalihārī nā ghar-vās base, pāṅde . Rus. Wom. Widowed be she! when will this mother-in-law be dead? See, holy father, in thy book! for while this wrangling jade's to live this house wont prosper, priest.
more: n. f. kulhāṛā , n. m. S. कुठारी An axe; a hatchet. kulhāṛ'yā , ( Bot .) Hatched-shaped.

def: s.m. See s.v. کلي kali. s.f. A woollen carpet; tapestry ( = qālīn).
more: n. m. S. कलयुग The 4th or iron age comprising a period of 432,000 years, supposed to have begun on Friday, the 18th of February, or 3,102 years before the Christian era. 1. Beṭā khāve, bāp lakhāve, Kaliug apnā bal dikhlāve . Prov. The son feeds well, the father just looks on, the iron age dominion hath. 2. Yā kal'jug kī kāmnī chaleṅ parchhāīn kī chhāṅ, Mūṅh pe pallo ger ke kareṅ ughāṛī bāṅh . Kunḍlī. Fair damsels of this iron age even of their shadows vain, Their faces though close veiled they keep their arms all bare remain. kal-jug kā parbhāo! The influence of the iron age.
s. m. The fourth or iron age, comprising a period of 432,000 years, which is supposed to have begun about 3,000 years before the Christian era.

def: s.f. Blackness; black spot; blacking; ink; lamp-black; soot, the smut which collects under pots, &c.; cloudiness, darkness, gloom; — a fault or flaw (in gold); the line of hair extending from the pudenda to the navel; — the small singing-bird Turdus macrourus (com. called shāmā); a female crow; a black curlew.
s.m. A curlew; — adj. Of or relating to kali-yug.
s.f. Price to be paid at a fixed period or by instalments

adj. (f. -ā or -ī), Relating to time, connected with time; (in Gram.) relating to tense; temporal; seasonable; periodical.

more: n. f. See kālī . Tuhi Kālikā, tu hī Bhavānī! terī māyā jag ne jānī .
n. prop. f. Name of the Hindū goddess. See گوري gaurī or کالي kālī.

def: adj. (f. -ā), Covered (with), full (of), filled; mixed, blended; impenetrable, impervious; — s.m. A large heap; a thicket; confusion; mud.

s.m. A Christian church; a synagogue.

subs: or kālī-kunḍ, s.m. The pit or whirlpool in the river Jamnā in which the serpent Kālī is said to have lived

ety: for A. قلية qalīyat, v.n. fr. قلی 'to cook, fry,' &c.

more: n. m. Boiled flesh or meat dressed with anything; a curry. qalyā chapātī , n. m. Qalyā and bread.

s. m. A dish, broiled flesh or meat dressed with any thing.

def: s.m. A small simple kind of pipe or ḥuqqa ( = kalī).
more: n. m. 1. Completion; fulfilment. Yeh Umā, shimbhu bivāh jo nar kaheīṅ, jo nar gāveīṅ. Kalliyān kāj, bivāh maṅgal, sarbadā such pāvaīṅ . Rāmāyan. 2. Prosperity ( khush-hālī ). 3. See ānaṅd. As kuh Nārad sumir Hari. Girjaheṅ dinh asish, Huī hai yeh kalliyān ab, sansaë tajo Gīrīsh! Ditto. 4. Salvation. Kalliyān ho! kalyān h. , v. n. 1. To thrive; prosper; flourish. 2. To go to the dogs; to be ruined, undone.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit कल्याण (kalyāṇa).

def: s.m. A pipe for smoking through water, a kind of ḥuqqa.
more: n. m. A kind of huqqah .
s. m. A machine for smoking tobacco with.

def: v.n. To bud, blossom; — to begin to be fledged (a bird); to get new feathers.
more: v. n. To blossom, to bloom, to bud.

def: adj. Malignant, ill-omened, whose curses prevail.
s.m. The black kite.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), 'Black-tongued,' inimical, malignant, whose curses prevail; — an evil-tongued person; one whose curses prevail.
adj. Malignant, ill-omened, whose curses prevail.
adj. (dialec.) = kal-jibhā, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), 'Black-tongued,' inimical, malignant, whose curses prevail; — an evil-tongued person; one whose curses prevail.
adj. (f. -īṅ), Dark-complexioned, sallow, tawny.

s.m. The viscous deposit of oily substances; sediment; dregs; paste; ear-wax; filth, dirt, ordure; foulness; sin; fraud, falsehood, deceit, hypocrisy; the beleric myrobalan, Terminalia bellerica; — adj. Sinful, wicked.
s.m. (dialec.), Sediment, &c. ( = kalk); lamp-black.
more: s. (dakh. i. q. دوا کا کاجل) Lampblack used as a medicine for the eyes(?).

def: s.f. A plume, &c. ( = kalg̠ī, q.v.); — (local) a kind of instrument for smoking with.
more: n. m. The 10th incarnation of Vishnu which is to come. The god will appear as a Brahman who will be born in the town of Sambal and in the family of Vishnu Sharinā. He will ride on a horse and put to death all the wicked.

s. m. The name of the tenth Hindū incarnation, which will happen. The deity will assume the appearance of a Brāhmaṇ, who is to be born in the town of Sambal and in the family of Vishnu Sarmā. He will ride on horseback and put to death all the wicked.
s. (dakh.) An instrument for smoking tobacco, &c. with: see Qanoon-e-islam, under the word mudud in the glossary.

def: s.m. Name of Vishṇu in his future capacity of destroyer of the wicked and liberator of the world from its enemies (this is to constitute the tenth or last avatār or descent of the deity, and is to take place at the end of the four yugas or ages; the god will appear as a Brāhman who will be born in the town of Sambhal and in the family of Vishṇu Śarmā).
s.m. A confused or muffled sound; bustle, stir, commotion, tumult.

ety: prob. fr. S. कल् 'to collect,' and akin to kalī
def: s.m. A bud, a sprout; a cabbage ( = karam-kallā); (met.) the head (see کله kalla)
more: s. m. 1. A cabbage. 2. (کله) The jaws, the head. کلا پايچه kallā pāʼecha, s. m. Name of a dish, the head and feet. کلا خشک kallā ḵẖushk, Lantern-jawed; ( met. ) treacherous, cunning, malicious. 3. Noise, tumult. کلا کار kallā-kār, adj. Noisy, tumultuous. کلا ٿهلا kallā ṭhallā, Pomp and splendour. ¶ kullā, s. (dakh.) See کلاه kulāh.
s. m. (dakh.) The cheek, jole, the gills (of a fish: Harris ).
subs: s.m. The head and feet (of an animal); name of a dish.
wety: From Sanskrit कील (kīl).

def: s.m. A dog-keeper.

more: adj. 1. Shrewd, malicious. 2. Wretched, friendless, cunning. 3. Addicted to drinking, a drunkard.

def: adj. = qallāć, q.v. adj. & s.m. Shrewd, cunning, crafty; malicious; — poor, wretched, friendless; — addicted to drinking; — a cunning or crafty person; a needy, worthless fellow; a poor wretch; — a drunkard, a haunter of taverns.
more: adj. Cor. of T. qallāsh . Poor (غريب). Muflis qallāṅch .

def: s. m. A kind of sweetmeat.

more: adj. Sterile; barren; unproductive. kallar , n. f. 1. A soapy soil; fuller's earth. Kallar kā khet, or kapṭī kā het . Rus. A soapy soil, the false heart's spoil. 2. Land likely to produce salt petre or reh . 3. ( Tir .) A class of men of no caste, or men who have lost their castes during a famine. kallar-khāī dhartī , A barren soil. kallar lagnā , v. n. 1. To be impregnated with alkali or reh . 2. To become barren (soil).
s. (dakh.) Thief, robber, highwayman, footpad ( Harris ).
subs: s.f. Land impregnated with saltpetre or reh, barren soil
v.n. To be impregnated with alkali or reh; to become barren (land).

def: adj. & s.m. Shrewd, cunning, crafty; malicious; — poor, wretched, friendless; — addicted to drinking; — a cunning or crafty person; a needy, worthless fellow; a poor wretch; — a drunkard, a haunter of taverns.
more: adj. 1. Shrewd, malicious. 2. Wretched, friendless, cunning. 3. Addicted to drinking, a drunkard.

def: s.m. Blackness; a black spot, stain, slur, blot; shame and confusion (as of a convicted offender); suspicion; calumny.

ety: for A. کلمة kalimat; see kalām
def: s.m. A word, speech, saying, discourse; a vocable, part of speech; the Mohammadan confession of faith (i.e. lā ilāha illaʼl-lāh, &c., 'There is no god but God,' &c.)
more: n. m. 1. A word ( bāt , 1). 2. ( Gram .) A part of speech. 3. The Mah. confession of faith, viz. 1. Lā illah, illiah, Mahammad ar rasūl allāh . There is but one God, and Mohammad is the apostle of God. 2. Merekalme ke gavāh' rahnā! (Said by a dying man). Bear witness to what I have said. kalmah paṛhānā , v. a. To convert to Mahomedanism. kalmah paṛhnā , v. n. 1. To repeat the kalmah , q. v. kalmah paṛhā aur pāk ! Repeat the kulma and be pure! 2. To acknowledge one's superiority; kiss the rod. 1. Sunā hāi, aë zanākhī, tū ne bājā jab se argan kā Lagī hai tab se kalmah paṛhne tū azkhud faraṅgan kā . Wom. Rangīn. Since on your ear sounded the organ reed You 're taken to repeat the Christian creed. 2. Tum to khānam se kyā miliṅ ki usi kā kalmah paṛhne lagīṅ? Mah. Wom. kalmat-ul-haq . Truth. kalmah-i-zabānī . Word of mouth. kalmah-i-kufr . Blasphemy; profanity. kalme kā sharīk' , A coreligioni…

s. m. 1. A word, speech, saying. 2. Part of speech. 3. The Muhammadan confession of faith. کلمة الحق kalimatu-l-ḥaḳḳ, The word of God. کلمه پڙهنا kalma paṛhnā, To make the Muhammadan confession of faith. کلمي کي انگلي kalme kī unglī, s. f. The forefinger. (Arab. plur. کلمات).
subs: (-ko), To make (one) repeat the (Muslim) confession of faith, to convert (one) to Mohammadanism, to make a Musalmān of (one)
To repeat the (Muslim) confession of faith; — to acknowledge the superiority (of); to say what another says, to follow servilely, to swear (by)
Blasphemous word or speech, blasphemy, profanity
s.m. A Musalmān or Muslim; — a well-wisher, one who sings the praises (of), or one who takes another as his oracle.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian کَلِمَه (kalima), from Arabic كَلِمَة (kalima).

def: s.f. See s.v. kal, 'ease,' &c. s.m. A saying, a word; loquaciousness; — boasting, egotism
subs: v.n. To talk, prate; to be very loquacious; — (with loc.) to rebuke, reproach
or qīl-o-qāl, s.m. Confabulation; logomachy; altercation, wrangling, squabbling.

def: s. f. Distraction, trouble.

def: (cf. kalol), s.f.(m.?) A heifer.

s.m. Sacred precept, rule, law, ordinance; rite, ceremony; performance, manner of acting, practice prescribed by the Vedas; the most complete of the six Vedāngas (comprehending the description of religious rites); a śāstra or sacred book of the Hindūs; — a resolve, resolution, determination, purpose; propriety, fitness; optionality, alternative; doubt; toxicology; — a fabulous period of time, a day of Brahma or 1000 yugas, a period of 4,320,000,000 years (measuring the duration of the world); the duration of the world; the destruction of the world; — an epithet of Vishṇu (as containing all the sacred precepts; — name of one of the trees of Indra's paradise (see kalp-taru and kalp-vr̤iksh)
more: s. m. 1. A Shāstra or sacred work. 2. A day or a night of Brahmā, a period of 4,320,000,000 years of mortals, measuring the duration of the world, and as many the interval of its annihilation. 3. A destruction of the world. 4. Optionality, doubt. 5. Resolve, purpose. 6. Propriety, fitness. کلپ برچهہ कल्पवृक्ष kalpa-brichh or -vr̤iksh̤a, or کلپتر कल्पतरु kalpa-taru, A tree in Indraʼs heaven that yields whatever may be desired.
subs: (˚pa+an˚), s.m. The end of a kalpa or period of the world's duration, the end of the world
s.m. & adv. Another kalp; — in the midst of a kalp; from kalp to kalp
s.m., or kalp-latā, s.f., or kalp-vr̤iksh, vulg. kalp-br̤iksh, s.m. One of the fabulous plants or trees of Indra's paradise, a tree that grants all desires
s.m. The final destruction, the end of the world.

def: s.f. Fashioning, fabrication; contrivance, scheme, plan, invention; imitation, forgery; — inference, supposition, hypothesis, conjecture; fancy; imagination, thought, conception; — keen or ardent desire; trouble, difficulty
subs: v.t. To fashion, form, make, fabricate, contrive, &c.; to suppose, &c.

def: v.t. To grieve, distress, pain.
more: v. a. To give pain. ( satānā ). 1. Māt pitā ne jo kalpāve uskā janam akārat jāve . Who causeth parents' tears to flow through all his life no good shall know. 2. Rāt dinā malṅ ṭuhal karūṅ thī, utṭā taiṅ kalpāyā, Maīṅ man mār rahī ghar apne, nā koī god khilāyā . Play. Chaubolā . By night and day I've served you well, with pain you've me repaid, My hopes all crushed at home I've stayed, no babe in my lap hath played.

more: v. n. See kalpānā , v. a.
n. f. Keen desire; ardent wish. kalpnā karnā , v. a. 1. To make a vow. 2. ( Geom .) To suppose.

s.m. Action, act, deed, work, doing, handiwork, performance; work, labour, duty, task, job; business, occupation, employment, office, function; operation, undertaking, transaction, affair, matter, thing, concern, interest; — a hard task, a difficult matter; a feat; object, end, purport; — workmanship; manufacture; needlework, embroidery; — service, use; serviceableness, fitness (for); need (of, -se), occasion (for); — the mail-packet, mails, post-bag
more: n. m. S. कर्म्म, Pālī, kammam ; Pr. kammo . See ادم 2, 3, and ارتهـ 6, 8). 1. Work; handiwork; job. 1. Jaise dām vaisā kām . Prov. As the price you pay the work you get. 2. Iskā kyā kām hai? What does he want? 3. Diankā ke khāil peṅṛ, soch ke māral deh kavno kām ke na rahe . Bhoj. Prov. A tree eaten by white ants, and a body worn with care are nothing worth. 2. Labor; task. 1. Āj/?/kal baṛā kām hai . 2. Chār āne roz kā kām kartā hai . 3. Performance; feat (عمل 2). 4. Skill; dexterity; art; tactics. Soch-ke chalnā, musafir! yahāṅ ṭhagoṅ kā gāoṅ hai, Is sarā meṅ hazāroṅ mare, is se bachnā kām hai , O traveller, walk with care! this is the Thag's estate, Slain thousands here have been, needs skill to shun their fate! 5. Workmanship; fine work; embroidery. Is rūmāl par achchhā kām hai . 6. Mechanism; manufacture. 7. Service; use. 8. Office; department; function; task; duty; part. Pardah-posh…

more: n. m. کمل completed. See pūrā-pan . (1, 2, 4, 5). 1. Perfection; excellence. 2. A wonder; miracle. Usko is fan meṅ hamāl lāsil hai. kamāl , adj. 1. Perfec. 2. Extreme. To succeed to perfection. Bhāī! tum ne to kamāl kīyā! 2. To overreach; outwit. kamā'ā , n. m. 1. Athletic exercise. 2. A sham wrestling match to show feats of skill.
(from کمل) s. m. 1. Perfection, excellence. 2. Completion, conclusion. 3. Punctuality. (Used adjectively) Complete, entire, perfect, very great. (Plur. کمالات).

n. f. 1. A bow. Kāl ke hāth kamān, bīṛhā bache na javān . Prov. Time ready stands armed with his bow, nor old nor young escape below. 2. An arch. 3. (A. qaus , S. धनु ) ( Ast .) Sagittarius. kamān , adj. 1. Flexible; elastic. 2. Curved; bent; bowed. kamān-abrū , Poet. Arched eyebrows. kamān utārnā , v. a. To unbend a bow. kamān-bardār , n. m. s. f. A bow. کمان ابرو kamān-abrū, With arched eyebrows (epithet of a mistress). کمان چڙهنا kamān chaṛhnā (dakh. met. i. q. غالب هونا) To be victorious, to overcome(?). کماندار kamān-dār, An archer, one armed with a bow. کمانگر kamān-gar, A bow-maker, a bonesetter.

ety: kamā˚ = kamāw˚ = Prk. कम्माव(इ) = S. कर्म + आपि, caus. augment
def: v.t. To earn, to acquire, get; to accumulate, save; to work, labour; to perform; to do (good); to perpetrate, commit (a crime); to exercise (the faculties) to clean or curry (leather); to clean (a privy, &c.); to dress or prepare (land)
more: v. a. S. कर्म 1. To earn; get. 1. Dharam kamānā . 2. Pāp kamānā . 3. Unhoṅ ne baṛā rupayā kamāyā . 4. Rupae ko rupayā kamātā hai . Prov. Money makes money. 5. Kamāveṅ Khān-khānāṅ. uṛāveṅ mīyāṅ fahīm . Prov. The master earns, the canny servant spends. 6. Kamāne khāne kī bāteṅ haiṅ . By such arts a living is made. 2. To work; labor; drudge. 3. To exercise (faculties); train; develope. 4. To clean or curry (leather). Chamṛā kamānā . 5. To clean a chamber pot. Mehtrānī gubār dil se jhāṛ! Kam kam-ānā terā qayāmat hai. Zilā . A pun. 6. To prepare the land; to enrich; fertilize. Zamīn kamānā . 7. To live by prostitution. Kharchī kamānā. kamā khānā , v. a. To live by one's own earnings. kamā lenā , v. n. To wear away; consume. Bīmārī ne kamā līyā .
v. a. 1. To earn. 2. To work, to perform, to perpetrate, to commit (crime), to do (good). 3. To clean (leather or a privy), to curry. 4. (from Pers. کم) To lessen, to abate.
subs: To live by one's own earnings.

ety: P. kam+ā = āw = Prk. आव or आवे = S. आपि (caus. augment) + nā
def: v.t. To lessen, diminish, abate; — v.n. To grow less; to become deficient
subs: v.t. (intens.) To reduce, wear away, consume.

subs: or kamand ḍālnā, or kamand lagānā (with loc.), To throw up a scaling ladder, to scale; — s.m. Scaling.

ety: Pehl. kamar; Zend kamara, fr. kamar = S. kmar
def: s.f. The loins, the waist; a girdle, zone, belt; the middle (of a mountain, &c.); the flank (of an army); an arch
more: n. f. 1. The loins; waist. 1. Māṅgne par kamar bāṅdh lī hai, mahnat nahīṅ ho saktī (Said to a beggar). You have girt your loins to beg, not to work. 2. Kālā hai, par kāg nahīṅ pātāl rahe, par nāg nahīṅ; Patlī kamar hai, chītā nahīṅ; uchak chaṛhe, laṅgūr nahīṅ . Riddle. Ans. The large black ant. It's black, a crow it's not; lives under ground, a snake it's not; It's waist is thin, a leopard not; it bounds, an ape it's not. 2. The middle. 3. A belt. kamar bāṅdhnā, yā kasnā , v. a. 1. To gird up one's loins; to arm. 2. To prepare. kamar-bastā , adj. Having the loins girt; armed; prepared. kamar-band , n. m. 1. A girdle; zone; belt; waistband. 2. A long piece of cloth girt round the loins. kamar-bandī , n. f. 1. Arming; preparation for battle, etc. 2. Military uniform. kamar pakaṛ-ke uṭhnā , v. n. To become weak or feeble. kamar pakaṛnā yā thāmnā , v. a. 1. To grip one by the waist. 2. To su…

s. f. 1. The loins, the waist. 2. A girdle, a belt, zone. 3. An arch. 4. The flank (of an army). 5. The middle (of a mountain). کمرباندهنا kamar bāndhnā, To begird; to get ready, to resolve, to be intent, to arm. کمربسته kamar-basta, adj. Having the loins girt; ready. کمربند kamar-band, 1. s. m. A girdle, a zone, waist-band. 2. adj. Alert, ready for battle, prepared, armed. کمربندي kamar-band-ī, s. f. Arming, preparation for battle, &c. کمربندي کرنا kamar-bandī karnā, To arm. کمربيٿهني kamar-baiṭhnī (dakh.) To become weak or dejected. کمرپکڙکي اٿهنا kamar pakaṛke uṭhnā, To be weak. کمرپکڙنا kamar pakaṛnā, To urge a claim against one. کمرٿوٿنا kamar ṭūṭnā, 1. To be hopeless. 2. To be deserted by oneʼs friends. کمرتوڙنا kamar toṛnā, 1. To take away oneʼs hopes. 2. To draw over oneʼs friends or adherents to the side of oneʼs enemies. کمر دوال kamar-doʼāl, A leathern belt. کمرره جانا kamar r…
subs: (-par), To gird up the loins (for), to get ready, prepare, arm (for); to resolve, to be intent (on)
adj. Having the loins girt, in a state of readiness, on the alert, ready for action, prepared, armed
adj. = kamar-basta; — s.m. A girdle, zone, sash, belt, waistband; a long piece of cloth folded round the loins; the string with which drawers, &c. are fastened (syn. izār-band)
(-kī), To encourage, inspirit, give hope of success (to), to stimulate (one to persevere)
s.f. Girding up the loins, preparing, getting ready; preparation (for action), prepared state, alertness
s.m. A zone, or cincture (of gold or silver); — a tie-beam
'To rise holding the loins'; to be in a weak or feeble state
or kamar thāmnā (-kī), To seize (one) by the waist; to urge a claim (against one); — to support, back, help, encourage
adj. (f. -ī), Gone in the loins; impotent; — hump-backed
(-kī), 'The loins or back to be broken'; to be deserted by (one's) friends; to give way or sink under the loss of a child, &c.; to be deprived of hope, to lose confidence or courage
(-kī), To break the back (of); to take away all hope (from), to discourage, &c.; to alienate one's friends or adherents
The loins or back to give way; to have a pain in the loins (from long standing); the back to become palsied
To stretch one self out for brief repose, to lie down
or kamar kasnā = kamar bāṅdhnā, q.v.

s.f. Ungirding the loins; undoing or opening the waistband; (Mil.) the order to take off accoutrements; — a fee levied by a government peon from a person over whom he is placed in charge for permission to perform the common functions of life
s.m. The part of a beam which projects outside a wall
To undo or throw off (one's) belt, &c.; to quit service; to take (one's) ease
(-ko), To have a sore back (a horse); to get a back-ache (through lying too long in bed)
(-kī), To strike sideways; to attack (an army) in flank
To take courage or heart; to undertake a work of expense
'To shake or move the loins'; to endeavour; — to copulate.

ety: Prk. कम्मावउओ or कम्मावेउओ; S. कर्मापयितृ+कः
def: s.m.f. Earning a livelihood; laborious, industrious; — worker, labourer; earner, bread-winner; husband
more: adj. Earning; bread-winning. kamāū khasam kis ne na chāhā? Prov. A bread-winning husband is every woman's desire.

n. m. 1. Earner; bread-winner. 2. A worker. 3. A husband. Rām jī kaṛe terā kamāū jīve ! Wom. Benediction. Rām grant the support of your house live long! kamāū pūt , W.; kamā-sut , E. n. m. A son who earns. Kamāū pūt kī dūr balā . Prov. A son who earns keeps off calamity. kamāū-dhan , n. m. 1. Male issue; sons. 2. Conveyances on hire; hackney coaches (Opp. of khātā-dhan , q. v.).
subs: A son who earns his living, a grown-up son
s.m. Male issue, sons; property, &c. by which one earns a living; conveyances (let out on hire), &c.

def: adj. Laborious.
adj. See. کماحقه
adj. (f. -trī), Desirous, cupidinous, lustful, libidinous.
adj. (f. -trī), Desirous, cupidinous, lustful, libidinous.
s.m. A blanket ( = kammal); a garment of wool
more: n. m. kamblī, kāmlī , n. f. S. कम्बल Pālī, kambalo . A coarse blanket. 1. Thoṛe mol kī kāmlī kare baṛoṅ kā kām, Mahmūdī aur bāftā sab ke rākhe mān . Dohā. A blanket cheap great service does, respect to silks and lace it shows. (It protects them from being soiled). 2. Faqīr apnī kamli hī meṅ khush hai . Prov. The beggar is happy in his blanket. 3. Tulsī! kārī kamrī chaṛhe na dūjā raṅg . Tulsī! the blanket black no other hue will take. kambal-posh , n. m. A mendicant whose only garment is a blanket. apnī kamlī meṅ mast h. , v. n. To be indifferent ( apnī khāl meṅ mast honā ).
subs: adj. & s.m. Wearing a blanket; — a mendicant whose only gament is a blanket.
wety: Borrowed from Punjabi کَمبَل (kambal).

def: (For this and other words beginning with the letters کنبـ that are not found below, see the corresponding Hindī forms beginning with کمبـ kamb˚.)

s.m. corr. of kāmod, q.v.

subs: adj. (f. -ā), Having a neck marked with three lines like a shell (held to indicate exalted fortune).

def: n. m. One who shirks his work; one who does not give his full tale of work. Kām-chor, nivāle hāzir . Prov. Work shirk, victuals jerk (down the throat)!
more: adj. (dakh.) Behindhand, skulker, idle fellow ( Harris ).

wety: Ultimately from Turkic (likely via Chagatai [Term?]). Compare Kipchak قمچی (kamçı).

def: s.m. An indolent person who does not turn out a proper amount of work; one who shirks work, a skulker.
H. P., n. m. Manager; agent ( kārkun ). kām-dār , adj. Worked; embroidered.

s. m. The god of love, the Indian Cupid. He was the son of Vish̤ṇu and Rukmiṇī, and husband of Rati (or Venus). Having offended Mahādeva, he was destroyed by the flame from the eyes of the angry Deity. He was, however, born again as Pradyumna, son of Kr̤ish̤ṇa; but was immediately seized by Sambara (whom it had been foretold he would destroy), and thrown into the ocean; where he was swallowed by a fish, which being caught was presented to Sambara; and, the child, on being found in the belly of the fish, was committed to Rati, who reared him, till he accomplished the prophesy in destroying Sambara. See more under رتي ratī.
s.m. = S کامروپ कामरूप kām-rūp, adj. See s.v. kāma or kām.
more: n. m. The god of love; the Indian Cupid; the son of Vishnu and Rukmanī , and the husband of Ratī or Venus.

def: n. 1. Indra's cow, who grants every thing asked of her. 2. A cow that gives much milk.
1. n. prop. Name of a cow, belonging to Indra, that grants every thing requested of her. 2. A cow that gives much milk.
s.m. A place of sacrifice; an altar; — a slaughter-house, shambles (syn. maslaḵẖ; maẕbaḥ).
s m. A workman; labourer, hireling, hired agricultural labourer; a journeyman; an assistant.
more: n. m. kameran , n. f. S. कर्मकर Pālī, kamakāro . A workman; laborer, esp. a hired agricultural laborer. Niyār, lāṅk, an deo baterā, jī sūṅ kār kare na kamerā . Rus. Feed the labourer eversomuch he'll never work with all his heart.
s. m. A workman, journeyman, an assistant.

ety: prob. S. कम्पय˚, caus. of कम्प्+ṛā = S. र+कः
def: s.m. Convulsion; — kameṛe, s.m. pl. Convulsions.
n. m. Convulsions. Mātā ke kameṛoṅ meṅ ā gayā .

n. f. A dove. Yūṅ taṛphūṅ jūṅ olan mārī taṛphe koī kameṛī rī, Khaṭmal ne khāl ucheṛī rī . Rus. Wom. As some poor hail-struck dove may fluttering lie, So flayed by bugs e'en toss and flutter I.
s.m. (dialec.) = kamerā, q.v. s m. A workman; labourer, hireling, hired agricultural labourer; a journeyman; an assistant.
s.f. Committee.
wety: Corruption of English committee (possibly through Punjabi کَمیٹی (kameṭī)).

def: adj. & s.m. See s.v. kām. s.m. Action, act, deed, work, doing, handiwork, performance; work, labour, duty, task, job; business, occupation, employment, office, function; operation, undertaking, transaction, affair, matter, thing, concern, interest; — a hard task, a difficult matter; a feat; object, end, purport; — workmanship; manufacture; needlework, embroidery; — service, use; serviceableness, fitness (for); need (of, -se), occasion (for); — the mail-packet, mails, post-bag
more: 1. adj. Effecting or obtaining whatever is desired, absolute, powerful, uncontrolled. 2. A beast or bird of chase that always catches its game.
subs: adj. Zealously following or pursuing a business; occupied, busy

v.n. To come into use; to come of use (to, -ke), be of use or service, prove serviceable (to); to be of avail, to avail, to stand in stead; to be wanted; to be used, or consumed, or spent (in, -meṅ); to fall, be killed or slain (in battle, &c.)
(-kā, or apnā), To increase the work (of); — to lay aside work, to cease from, or give over, work
(-kā), To spoil or mar the work (of); to make a mess of a business, &c.; to put a spoke in (one's) wheel; to baffle, to foil; to ruin (one's) reputation or credit
v.n. A work to be begun; a workshop to be opened, a business to be started or established
v.n. To go to work; to go on duty
v.n. To be at work; to be engaged in work, to have a post or employment, be in office
v.n. To come or be of use, to prove of service ( = kām ānā); to have to do (with, -se), to have business (with), to require, be in need (of), have occasion (for)
(or -par) lagānā (-ko), To set (one) to work, give (one) work to do; to give (one) an employment, to employ, take into service
(-kā), To finish a work or business, accomplish a task; to settle the business (of), to put an end (to one), to despatch, to kill
(-kā), A work or task to be finished, &c.; the business (of a person) to be settled, to be put an end (to), to be killed
(-kā), To put a work or job in hand; to set a (machine, &c.) to work
(apnā), To keep a work going; to further or advance a work or business; to manage to do (with, -meṅ)
adj. Sufficient to go on with; answering as a make-shift; serviceable
s.m. One who shirks work; one who does not give his full tale of work; a skulker, an idle fellow
adj. Worked (with, -kā); having any particular kind of work; embroidered; — s.m. Man of business, manager, agent, steward
s.f. A kind of embroidery work on net or muslin (small flowers worked in gold or silver thread)
(-kā), To inspect the work (of), to overlook, supervise
(-ko), To give work or employment (to), to employ; to make over office or charge (to); to render good service (to, -kā), to work well (for)
(-se), To have business or work (with), to have to do (with), to occupy oneself (with); to be connected (with)
(-ko), To give (one) work to do; to make over charge of an office ( = kām denā)
v.n. To be or become unfit for use, to become unserviceable, be past work; to be removed from office or employment
v.t. To bring into operation or use, to make use of, to apply
adj. (f. -ī), Of work, working, busy (e.g. kām-kā din); of use, useful, of service, serviceable, of importance (to, -se); of need
adj. & s.m. Laborious, industrious, busy, active; managing, superintending; — a worker, laborious or industrious man; manager, superintendent

To do work, to be busy; to act; to engage in practical affairs; to mind (one's own, apnā) business; to accomplish a business; to act or operate (upon, -par), to be of use (to), to take effect (on), be effectual, to succeed; to settle the business (of, -kā), to kill, destroy
To begin a work, to take or put a work in hand
To take in work, to undertake a job; to take over charge of an office (from, -se); to work (one, or a beast, &c.), to make use (of, -se), to use
To obtain an employment, or a post, &c.
To shirk work, to skulk; to work in a dilatory manner
To kill two birds with one stone
(-ko), To bring into use, to use, to employ; to apply; to expend
(-se), To carry into effect, to effect (one's) purpose (by means of), to accomplish (one's) desire; to get (one's) work done
(-se), Work to be got out (of); things to be gained, or to result
adj. Without work, unemployed, unoccupied; vacant; unused, lying idle.

ety: kam, q.v.+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Littleness, scantiness; deficiency; deficit; paucity, scarcity, dearth, lack, want; decrease, diminution, abatement, reduction; fall (of prices); remission; loss
more: n. f. 1. Deficiency; scantiness. 2. Dearth; paucity; scarcity: want; lack. 3. Loss; decrease; waste. 4 Reduction; diminution; abatement. 5. Defalcation; deficit. 6. Remission; relaxation. kamī beshī , n. f. 1. Excess and deficiency; inequality. 2. Fluctuation; profit and loss. kamī beshī barābar haī . Inequality made up. kami-jāëdād . Deficiency of assets. kamī k. , v. a. To fall short ( kam k ).
s. f. Deficiency, littleness, paucity, loss, abatement, diminution, decrease.
subs: s.f. Decrease and increase, excess and deficiency; fall and rise, fluctuation; profit and loss; (in revenue) an abstract account of the increase and decrease of the rental of each cultivator in a village.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian کمی (kamī).

ety: act. part. of کمل 'to be complete,' &c.; see kamāl
def: adj. & s.m. Perfect, complete; full, plenary; whole, entire; positive, decisive, absolute; — learned, accomplished, proficient, skilled, adroit, expert; — a perfect man; a saint; a proficient, a master, &c.
more: adj. See pūrā 3, 5. 1. Whole; full. 2. Accomplished ( fāzil , 2). 3. Positive; decisive; absolute. kāmil-ul-qīmat . Of full or adequate value. kāmil jamٴa , The highest assessment leviable. kāmil h. , v. n. 1. To be completed, finished. 2. To master; make oneself master. ijrāë kāmil , G. G. Complete execution. ikhtiyārāt-i-kāmil . G. G. Full powers. shaë kāmil . G. G. The entire thing.
n. m. A perfect man; a saint. Ishq kyā shaë hai, kisī kāmil se pūchhā chāhiye ?
m. (part. act. of کمال) adj. 1. Perfect, complete, accomplished. 2. Learned. 3. Entire.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian کامل (kāmil), from Arabic كَامِل (kāmil).
